title: Weberbauera densiflora
taxonomy: Weberbauera densiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the alpine regions of New Zealand. The plant is classified as part of the genus Weberbauera, which is a group of flowering plants that were previously classified as part of the genus Cardamine. Weberbauera densiflora is closely related to other species within the Brassicaceae family, such as those in the genera Cardamine and Lunaria. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this species.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential antiviral and anticancer activities.
